changesProposed.name: Engine Ventures

verified.name: Engine Ventures
verified.thesis: Engine Ventures invests in early-stage Tough Tech companies developing hardware-based technologies across fields including biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing.
verified.notes: In 2023, the investment fund separated from The Engine nonprofit organization and rebranded as the venture capital firm Engine Ventures. The Engine is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit founded by MIT in 2016; Engine Ventures is the separate VC arm. Current database entry lists founded year as 2023 for Engine Ventures specifically, which is correct per source [3]. AUM and flagship fund name could not be verified from provided sources. Key partners Katie Rae and Reed Sturtevant could not be verified as current senior partners from firm website or Wikipedia. Source [4] is about SpaceX acquiring Cursor and is not relevant to Engine Ventures.

changes.current: Engine Ventures invests in early-stage deep tech companies developing hardware-based technologies across fields including biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing.
changes.verified: Engine Ventures invests in early-stage Tough Tech companies developing hardware-based technologies across fields including biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing.
